PLUG INTO DATA TO INVESTIGATE UTILITIES

Tom McGinty Newsday January/February 2007
Data & DatabasesInvestigative Methods

Tom McGinty analyzed Long Island Power Authority data to reveal insights into power plant utilization and wasteful spending on new facilities. The story demonstrates the value of database reporting in uncovering utility inefficiencies and regulatory issues.

Tom McGinty explored power demand on Long Island by analyzing a database obtained from the Long Island Power Authority. Despite initial resistance from LIPA officials, the story unveiled fresh insights into power plant utilization and unnecessary public expenditure on new power plants. McGinty advised on the wealth of data available regarding utility regulation and competition, emphasizing database utilization for thorough reporting on energy issues.
